items.title: AI is helping rangers protect Victorian wildlife in a fraction of the time

children.content: A new AI tool will help Parks Victoria manage native and endangered species affected by natural disasters, including the summer bushfires, with the department making the technology available to everyone.

gallery.altText: A green square frames a small brown mouse-like animal that stands on hind legs beside a pole in grassland.

thumbnail.altText: A green square frames a small brown mouse-like animal that stands on hind legs beside a pole in grassland.

items.title: Promise of $1b to fix Victorian roads in upcoming budget

children.content: The funding package also includes nearly $37 million dedicated to cleaning up graffiti along major roads.

image.altText: A close up of a pothole.

items.title: Maccas wins battle to set up in 'world's coolest street'

children.content: The fast food giant has been cleared to set up an outlet in the Melbourne suburb of Northcote after successfully challenging a council decision to block its restaurant plans.

image.altText: An architectural rendering of a McDonald's store on High Street in Northcote.

items.title: Farmers examine sheep for magic numbers to navigate drought

children.content: With a quick feel of a sheep's back, farmers score their flock, finding the magic numbers to help them navigate record-breaking drought, and now diesel price spikes.

image.altText: A man in a shed feels a sheep's back

items.title: Club snaps four-year losing streak in country football thriller

children.content: Gormandale players are over the moon after breaking a 65-game losing streak on the weekend.

image.altText: Two smiling young men wearing black and yellow football jerseys

items.title: Man beats Guinness World record after running a half-marathon in stilts

children.content: Santiago Vergara, 33, hopes to have nabbed a Guinness World Record after running the Ballarat half-marathon on stilts in a time of 2 hours and 34 minutes.

gallery.altText: A man in stilts with a flag

thumbnail.altText: A man in stilts with a flag

items.title: Around 1,700 women at risk of family violence to be given home security boost

children.content: The Victorian government has announced women facing the danger of family violence will be able to upgrade their home security under a $3.5 million pledge.

image.altText: A CCTV camera and alarm system on a brick wall.

items.title: Australia to sell fleet of Bushmaster armoured vehicles to the Dutch

children.content: Australian Bushmaster armoured vehicles are being sent to the Netherlands following a request to Australia by the Dutch government.

image.altText: A Bushmaster sporting Ukrainian flags stands in front of a Globemaster airplane on the tarmac

items.title: Housing developer Assemble slashes number of promised affordable homes

children.content: Victorian planning authorities will allow Assemble to significantly reduce the number of affordable homes offered at large developments in Melbourne's north by more than half.

image.altText: An apartment building under construction

items.title: Police shoot at car amid chaotic night across Melbourne

children.content: Police are investigating multiple incidents overnight including a drive-by shooting, suspected firebombing and an allegedly violent attack on a tram.

image.altText: Firefighters stand on the street.

items.title: 'What crime did we commit?': Indigenous leaders call for respect after Anzac booing

children.content: Indigenous elders face racist booing from crowd members at dawn services in Melbourne, Sydney and Perth, with one man arrested and charged over his alleged involvement and others issued with move-on orders.

articles.title: What Anzac Day means to those who have served Australia

children.content: Australians taking part in Anzac Day commemorations share stories of sacrifice and bravery, and what today means to them.

image.altText: Female army officer wearing hat and chin strap

articles.title: 'Carry the hope': Thousands gather for Anzac Day service in Canberra

children.content: Prime Minister Anthony Albanese and New Zealand High Commissioner Andrew Needs are among those to gather in Canberra for the 111th anniversary of the Gallipoli landings.

image.altText: The facade of the Australian War Memorial was lit up before dawn on Anzac Day 2026.

articles.title: Ben Roberts-Smith speaks after Anzac Day event in Queensland

children.content: Ben Roberts-Smith shook hands and posed for photos with fellow attendees after the service on the Gold Coast wrapped up.

image.altText: Ben Roberts-Smith shakes hands with supporters in a crowd on a rainy day.

gallery.altText: People gathering for an Anzac Day service in Sydney's Martin Place

thumbnail.altText: People gathering for an Anzac Day service in Sydney's Martin Place

items.title: Victoria offers 20pc car rego discount in cost-of-living measure

children.content: Motorists will have two months to claim a 20 per cent rebate on car registration as the government attempts to counter price rises at the petrol pump.

image.altText: A licence plate

items.title: Summery days not over for Melbourne with warm run expected to stretch into May

children.content: A blocking high has put Melbourne on track for its longest run of warm April days in more than a decade.

image.altText: Birds fly over a river which is reflecting the sun with a bridge at the rear of the shot and city buildings either side.

items.title: Surfer turns decommissioned wind turbine blades into surfboard fins

children.content: At the Waubra Wind Farm near Ballarat, 40-metre turbine blades that once helped power the state are given a second life.

gallery.altText: Man in a wetsuit holding a surfboard at the beach.

thumbnail.altText: Man in a wetsuit holding a surfboard at the beach.

items.title: Vic govt announces $860m for 7,000 extra social housing properties

children.content: An extra 7,000 social housing properties will be built in Victoria in the coming decade, with an initial $860 million investment in the state budget, the Allan government has announced.

avatar.alt: A headshot of ABC reporter Rachel Eddie.

items.title: Threatened fish rescued in Victoria's 'most flow-stressed river'

children.content: A small fish that smells like freshly-cut cucumber could be akin to a canary in the coalmine for one of Victoria's most stressed rivers.

image.altText: xxx

items.title: Footballer returns to field after 15 days in induced coma

children.content: A Gippsland footballer who spent 15 days in an induced coma after contracting a rare nerve condition has made a remarkable return to the field.

image.altText: A photo of a male football player in a white, blue, red and black singlet and blue football shorts, on a football field

items.title: Centenarian bugler prepares to sound Last Post for 92nd time

children.content: A Boort centenarian prepares to sound his bugle this Anzac Day at local schools and the RSL.

image.altText: A 100-year-old man holding a bugle

items.title: Three men arrested after alleged arson attack in Melbourne CBD

children.content: Police were led on a foot-chase by alleged arsonists after a bar was set alight in ACDC lane early on Saturday morning.

image.altText: Bar Bambi arson

items.title: AFL sacks appeals boss after homophobia statement labelled 'disgusting'

children.content: Will Houghton KC is sacked as the chair of the AFL appeals board after the body's widely criticised statement and decision in the case of St Kilda player Lance Collard.

image.altText: The AFL logo on the side of a building, partially obscured by a tree branch.

items.title: Couple rebuilds home after bushfire on condition it will never burn again

children.content: During the Black Summer bushfires, the Grahams watched their house burn from a peephole in a fire bunker. When they decided to rebuild, they created the most fire-resilient home possible. Here's how they did it.

image.altText: A woman looks fondly at a man with fire, house plans and a new house build montage in the background.

items.title: The legacy of once-banned soldiers that hides in plain sight

children.content: Thousands of Indian soldiers served alongside Anzac forces at Gallipoli, leaving their legacy in the "puggaree" — a traditional Indian headwrap that was adapted to adorn the slouch hats worn by the Australian army.

image.altText: Six men with red and white striped turbans in khaki uniforms with red and white around their waist and long white socks.

items.title: 'Homophobia has no place': AFL boss rejects ruling on slurs in sport

children.content: Both the AFL and the AFL Players' Association hit back at suggestions from the appeals board that slurs are commonplace among players in the sport.

image.altText: Andrew Dillon standing next to AFL trophy

items.title: Viva Energy boss says more refineries needed for secure fuel supply

children.content: The Viva Energy refinery provides about 10 per cent of Australia's fuel. Its chief, Scott Wyatt, chats to Alan Kohler about the company's future in the wake of the Geelong fire.

articles.title: How Aussies near you are coping with the fuel crisis

children.content: Petrol prices are reshaping Australian life in ways big and small — from a grandmother's isolation to a farmer's future.

image.altText: Three people who are affected by high fuel prices.

items.title: Man, 72, charged with decade-old murder of football great's brother

children.content: Victoria Police charge a Colac man with murder as part of its investigation into the 2012 suspicious disappearance of St Kilda football champion Barry Breen's brother, Brendan Breen.

image.altText: Two photo of Brendan Breen, one in which he is posing for what looks like a police photo and another of him smiling.

items.title: Two charged after $13m drug seizure on Hume Highway

children.content: A man and woman in their 20s have appeared in court after allegedly being found with 33 kilograms of methylamphetamine.

image.altText: A blue and white Victoria Police sign outside HQ in Melbourne.

items.title: Police dismiss claims organised crime linked to farmer's murder

children.content: Fake images and false claims about Richard Wills's death are circulating on social media, prompting police to speak out about the investigation into the 65-year-old farmer's murder.

image.altText: Man wearing black cap holding a baby

items.title: Arsons at licensed venues continue to baffle Victoria Police

children.content: Police say none of the owners of venues recently targeted by arsonists have reported any extortion attempts, leaving investigators puzzled as to why they're being targeted.

image.altText: A restaurant.

items.title: Sheraz risked his life translating in Afghanistan. His family paid the price

children.content: Sheraz worked with Australian and US troops in Afghanistan, as a result his family were targeted by the Taliban. This Anzac Day he's being heroed.

gallery.altText: A man seated at a table, smiling.

thumbnail.altText: A man seated at a table, smiling.

items.title: Hundreds of trucks rumble past this school. It sacked its crossing supervisor

children.content: Parents at a Victorian school are calling for a crossing supervisor to be reinstated along a busy road, saying it is a matter of time until a child is killed.

image.altText: A lady with blonde hair and a pink t-shirt stands in front of a school zone

items.title: Ancient fossils reveal when the iconic Twelve Apostles formed

children.content: An analysis of tiny fossils of sea creatures found in rock samples around the Port Campbell region in south-western Victoria has refined our understanding of how the Twelve Apostles formed.

image.altText: Four limestone monoliths of varying size lapped at by shoreline waves next to massive cliffs.

items.title: 'Genuinely speechless': Appeal board's stunning claim in homophobia case

children.content: The appeal board in the Lance Collard homophobia case says homophobic and racist language is commonplace in the AFL due to the "highly competitive" nature of the game.

image.altText: Lance Collard stands with both arms in the air during a St Kilda vs West Coast Eagles AFL match in 2025.

items.title: New grapevine virus GINV detected for first time in Australia

children.content: A grapevine virus known in China and Japan that affects plant growth and productivity has been detected in Australia for the first time in Victorian, Queensland and South Australian table grapes.

image.altText: Undergrowth of table grape vines.

items.title: They answered the call, then Australia turned them away. But one digger fights on

children.content: No family in the world comes close to the fighting dynasty of Ricky Morris's. So, why are they still denied land owed generations ago?

gallery.altText: A man with medals stands on a wrecked church site

thumbnail.altText: A man with medals stands on a wrecked church site

items.title: Daylesford intersection where five people died to become green space

children.content: An intersection in a country Victorian town where five people died will be closed after $1 million in funding was offered as part of the Black Spots Program.

image.altText: A pub with tables and umbrellas in the front, with a large tree creating shade.
